completedActions.description: Documented how JS Buffer can be encoded in Avro: (1) bytes type = [varint-length][raw-bytes] ~17-18 bytes for 16-byte UUID, (2) string type = [varint-length][utf-8-bytes] ~37-38 bytes for UUID string. No other encoding methods. Bytes is wire-efficient and optimal for binary UUIDs.
completedActions.name: Analyze Avro binary encoding nuances

completedActions.description: All 4 references reviewed: identifiable.mjs stores UUID v7 string, forma.mjs SCHEMA has id:string, uuid64.ts TypeScript Buffer class exists but unused, tools/index.mjs and nameforma/index.mjs exports checked. UUID64 exists but not integrated.
completedActions.name: Review all task references and current codebase state

completedActions.description: Identified 7 components requiring changes: Forma.SCHEMA (1 line), Identifiable class (major refactor), conversion methods (3 methods), Schema.toAvro() (Buffer handling), package exports (2 files), test files (4 files with string ID assertions)
completedActions.name: Map scope implications across codebase

completedActions.description: UUID64.fromString() handles UUIDv7 strings perfectly (uuid64.ts:85-101). Recommend transparent conversion: Identifiable accepts string param, converts via UUID64.fromString(), stores as Buffer internally. This keeps API backward-compatible while changing internal representation.
completedActions.name: Clarify UUID64 integration approach

completedActions.description: No inherent need for UUID64 validation in Identifiable constructor. Validation is optional, currently done at Forma level via validate() method. Skip constructor validation unless defensive checks required. UUID64 validation would need wrapper to handle string format.
completedActions.name: Determine UUID64 validation requirement

completedActions.description: Current: ^1.12.0, Latest: 1.12.1 (patch, 4 months old). Update to ^1.12.1 in pkg/nameforma/package.json:39. No breaking API changes—type.toBuffer()/fromBuffer() remain stable.
completedActions.name: Update avro-js dependency

completedActions.description: UUID64.validate(input: string | Buffer) at uuid64.ts:253-289 validates both string and Buffer formats. Replace uuidValidate(f3a.id) calls with UUID64.validate(f3a.id) in forma.mjs:30 test. Update Avro schema assertions from 'string' to 'bytes' type in forma.mjs:66 and schema.mjs:91. Serialization round-trip tests unchanged.
completedActions.name: Clarify test validation approach using UUID64.validate()
Update UUID64.fromString() to validate input and throw on invalid

references.text: Identifiable at pkg/nameforma/src/identifiable.mjs:13-46 - refactor constructor: accept string/Buffer, use UUID64.fromString() for transparent conversion, store as #id Buffer. Expose asV7()/toDate()/compare() methods.
Forma.SCHEMA at pkg/nameforma/src/forma.mjs:38-48 - defines id field as 'string' type, change to 'bytes' for wire efficiency

UUID64 at pkg/tools/src/uuid64.ts:52-343 - fromString(input) factory (line 85-101) handles both UUID strings and base64. Methods: asV7() (string), toDate(), compare(), validate(). Ready for integration, no changes needed.
Schema.toAvro() at pkg/nameforma/src/schema.mjs:68-89 - uses type.clone() for serialization, may need Buffer handling
Test files: forma.mjs (77-82 avro test), identifiable.mjs, schema.mjs, uuid64.mjs - all expect string IDs, require updates
Package exports: nameforma/index.mjs:1-35 (missing UUID64), tools/index.mjs:1-2 (UUID64 already exported)
Avro bytes encoding: varint-prefixed binary (17-18 bytes for 16-byte UUID) vs string encoding (37-38 bytes for UUID string)
avro-js dependency at pkg/nameforma/package.json:39 - update from ^1.12.0 to ^1.12.1 (latest patch, no breaking changes)

summary: Integrate UUID64 binary storage with Avro serialization - use bytes type instead of string for optimal binary transmission
